commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A focused automated repair can align explicit false semantics and tests; maintainer/security acceptance is still needed after the repair because the PR changes an SSRF trust boundary.; Needs attention: The diff has no supply-chain concern, but it still appears to ignore an explicit private-network denial on an SSRF boundary.; Review finding: [P1] Honor explicit provider denial — src/plugins/openai-compatible-... (sha=7e6cb03a492fc92f0a5589d08308622a80def9f3)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `request.allowPrivateNetwork` to the configured OpenAI-compatible embedding provider shape, overlays `allowPrivateNetwork` onto the embedding SSRF policy when true, and adds regression coverage for private/exact-host behavior.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +101. Total +118 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main does not propagate configured provider request policy into the OpenAI-compatible embedding SSRF policy, and the linked issue describes the affected `memory status --deep` / indexing path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused automated repair can align explicit false semantics and tests; maintainer/security acceptance is still needed after the repair because the PR changes an SSRF trust boundary.

target.title: fix #94166: memory-core OpenAI-compatible embeddings: honor provider request.allowPrivateNetwork
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `memory-core` OpenAI-compatible embedding requests did not carry the selected configured provider's `request.allowPrivateNetwork` trust bit into the embedding guarded-fetch policy, so provider-level private-network trust was not honored on the memory embedding path.
- **Solution:** Keep the existing exact-host SSRF policy for OpenAI-compatible embedding base URLs and overlay `allowPrivateNetwork: true` only when the selected configured provider explicitly sets `models.providers.<id>.request.allowPrivateNetwork: true`.
- **What changed:** `ConfiguredEmbeddingProvider` now reads `request.allowPrivateNetwork`; OpenAI-compatible memory embeddings continue to build `allowedHostnames` from the configured base URL; explicit provider opt-in adds `allowPrivateNetwork: true`; tests cover opt-in policy construction, exact-host compatibility without broader opt-in, metadata/link-local rebinding blocking, and configured provider HTTP embedding routing through the gateway fixture.
- **What did NOT change:** Other embedding providers, non-memory model-provider request paths, gateway/channel/session routing, global SSRF helpers, schema/config surface, existing exact-host trust for configured/local provider aliases, and metadata/link-local protections without broader opt-in are unchanged.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Existing configured OpenAI-compatible memory provider aliases pointing at local/RFC1918 endpoints keep working after upgrade, and operators who intentionally need the broader provider private-network trust boundary can use the existing provider `request.allowPrivateNetwork` knob instead of adding a memory-only config surface or localhost proxy workaround.
- Fixes #94166

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — independent root cause.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- **Linked PR(s):** #94180 (`feat(memory-core): allow private network endpoints for memory embeddings`). The public-contract helper also flagged #92307 through an environment/version term, but #92307 is about exec-approval startup warnings and is not the same embedding/private-network contract.
- **Gap in linked PR(s):** #94180 adds a new memory-specific config surface, `memorySearch.remote.network.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork`, for a trust decision that the selected model provider can already express with `models.providers.<id>.request.allowPrivateNetwork`.
- **Why this patch is better/canonical:** This patch reuses the existing provider transport-trust contract as the canonical source of truth for configured providers instead of introducing a second memory-only knob, while preserving the shipped exact-host upgrade behavior for configured aliases.
- **Local real behavior proof advantage:** The local proof exercises the real `memory status --deep --json` CLI path against a loopback/private OpenAI-compatible endpoint twice: a no-opt-in configured alias succeeds through exact-host trust with one `/v1/embeddings` request, and the same configured provider with `request.allowPrivateNetwork: true` also succeeds with one `/v1/embeddings` request.
- **Related open PR scan:** Open PR searches for `request.allowPrivateNetwork`, `memory-core allowPrivateNetwork`, and OpenAI-compatible private embedding endpoints found #94180 as the same-contract alternative; no broader canonical provider-inheritance PR was found besides the current PR.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw memory status --deep` against a configured OpenAI-compatible embedding endpoint on a loopback/private address must preserve existing exact-host compatibility without `request.allowPrivateNetwork`, and must also honor the selected provider's explicit `request.allowPrivateNetwork: true` overlay.
- **Real environment tested:** Live CLI proof was re-captured after the RF-005 exact-host fix in the local PR worktree on Linux, using the real source CLI, the real `memory-core` CLI command, isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, and a local OpenAI-compatible `/v1/embeddings` HTTP endpoint bound to a loopback/private address with the host and port redacted below.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** The same command was run twice with the same endpoint and separate isolated configs: first without `models.providers.private-embedding-proof.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, then with `request.allowPrivateNetwork: true`.

```sh
node scripts/run-node.mjs memory status --agent proof --deep --json
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Exact-host compatibility result from the real CLI, without provider `request.allowPrivateNetwork`:

```json
{
  "agentId": "proof",
  "status": {
    "provider": "openai-compatible",
    "requestedProvider": "private-embedding-proof",
    "model": "text-embedding-3-small"
  },
  "embeddingProbe": {
    "ok": true
  }
}
```

Exact-host compatibility summary:

```json
{
  "code": 0,
  "containsEmbeddingsReady": true,
  "serverRequestCount": 1
}
```

Provider opt-in overlay result from the real CLI:

```json
{
  "agentId": "proof",
  "status": {
    "provider": "openai-compatible",
    "requestedProvider": "private-embedding-proof",
    "model": "text-embedding-3-small"
  },
  "embeddingProbe": {
    "ok": true
  }
}
```

Redacted endpoint request observed once in each case:

```json
{
  "method": "POST",
  "url": "/v1/embeddings",
  "headers": {
    "host": "<loopback>:<redacted-port>",
    "authorization": "<redacted>",
    "content-type": "application/json",
    "x-proof": "openclaw-pr-94344"
  },
  "body": {
    "model": "text-embedding-3-small",
    "input": ["ping"],
    "dimensions": 3
  }
}
```

Provider opt-in overlay summary:

```json
{
  "code": 0,
  "containsEmbeddingsReady": true,
  "serverRequestCount": 1
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The no-opt-in configured provider alias preserves exact-host behavior and reaches the loopback/private embedding endpoint successfully. The opt-in config also returns `embeddingProbe.ok: true`, while regression coverage confirms the client SSRF policy includes `allowPrivateNetwork: true` only for that explicit provider opt-in.
- **What was not tested:** I did not use a maintainer's real private LAN/VPN embedding service or third-party credentials. I used a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint on a loopback/private address to exercise the same guarded-fetch memory CLI surface. I did not drive a live metadata/link-local endpoint for safety; metadata/link-local rebinding remains covered by the regression test that resolves an exact-host policy to `169.254.169.254` and expects `Blocked: resolves to private/internal/special-use IP address` when `allowPrivateNetwork` is not set. I did not run `openclaw memory index --force`; `memory status --deep` is the requested memory runtime probe path and reaches the embedding availability check directly.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

| Finding | Status | Resolution |
| --- | --- | --- |
| RF-001 | Addressed | This update is the author-side response to the current `status: ⏳ waiting on author` label. |
| RF-002 | Fixed/proven | Preserved configured-provider exact-host trust: a no-opt-in configured local/private alias now succeeds through the real `memory status --deep` CLI path and through the gateway configured-provider fixture. |
| RF-003 | Disclosed | This intentionally extends the existing provider `request.allowPrivateNetwork` trust bit to memory embedding fetches; maintainer/security acceptance is still required for that trust-boundary policy. |
| RF-004 | Disclosed | Human maintainer/security review remains the final gate for the SSRF trust-boundary decision; this PR supplies the code change, compatibility preservation, and real behavior proof for that decision. |
| RF-005 | Fixed/proven | The embedding SSRF policy now always starts from `ssrfPolicyFromHttpBaseUrlAllowedHostname(baseUrl)` and only overlays `allowPrivateNetwork: true` for explicit provider opt-in; regression coverage locks exact-host local alias success and metadata/link-local blocking without broader opt-in. |

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test files:** `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts`; `src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** Configured provider with `request.allowPrivateNetwork: true` sets `client.ssrfPolicy.allowedHostnames === ["llm.internal"]` and `client.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork === true`; no-opt-in configured provider keeps `allowPrivateNetwork` unset, preserves exact-host trust, and still blocks metadata/link-local rebinding; configured local/private provider aliases work without a new opt-in; the gateway configured-provider route reaches the local generic embedding provider without adding `request.allowPrivateNetwork` to the fixture.
- **Commands run after RF-005 fix:** `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts --reporter=verbose`; `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
- **Observed local result:** `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts` passed 28/28 tests; `src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts` passed 14/14 tests in the local PR worktree after the RF-005 exact-host fix.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The unit tests pin the owner module's SSRF policy construction and metadata/link-local boundary, the gateway test pins the configured provider HTTP embedding route, and the live CLI proof pins the runtime memory-core guarded-fetch path. Together they cover the exact source-of-truth boundary without adding a broader fixture or changing unrelated provider paths.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** security-boundary, auth-provider, compatibility, session-state.
- **Risk explanation:** This touches the security boundary for private-network embedding fetches and the provider/config contract that decides when broader private-network trust is allowed. A wrong change could relax SSRF blocking by default, break existing configured local/private aliases, or create a second incompatible memory-only trust surface.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff is limited to the OpenAI-compatible embedding provider and its tests; shipped exact-host trust for configured aliases is preserved; metadata/link-local rebinding remains blocked without broader opt-in; broader private-network access stays behind explicit provider-scoped opt-in; no schema, migration, or new public config knob is introduced.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The provider `request.allowPrivateNetwork` contract is the existing source-of-truth for configured provider private-network transport trust, but the memory OpenAI-compatible embedding adapter built its SSRF policy from the embedding base URL without consuming that configured provider request state, so explicit broader provider trust was not reflected on memory embedding fetches.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The change moves the trust decision into the embedding client builder where the SSRF policy is created: the base policy remains exact-host `allowedHostnames`, and provider opt-in is the only source that overlays broader `allowPrivateNetwork` trust. This fixes the source policy construction instead of masking the blocked fetch downstream or adding a parallel memory-only override.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High — the patch stays focused on the embedding provider policy and configured-provider embedding test coverage, includes real CLI proof for the runtime path, preserves existing alias compatibility, and leaves the final product/security acceptance explicitly called out for maintainer review.
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch-quality warnings are expected and reviewed: the `unknown as EmbeddingProviderCreateOptions["config"]` casts are test fixture casts for partial config objects, not production type suppression; the `return undefined` paths preserve existing invalid/non-HTTP base URL behavior when no SSRF policy can be derived, not a fallback that permits private access. No skipped tests, `.only`, lint disables, or runtime broad catch were added.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The canonical contract surface is `models.providers.<id>.request.allowPrivateNetwork` for provider transport trust. This patch makes memory embedding SSRF policy consume that source-of-truth contract before guarded fetch runs; it does not change schema/default behavior, does not add migration or partial override semantics, and intentionally leaves other providers and non-memory request paths out of scope.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
